Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: Results discuss altered flavonol glycoside pattern caused by ugt78d2.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: ugt78d2 reduces 3-O-glucoside derivatives k2, k3, q2 and q3, consistent with the kaempferol/quercetin 3-O-glucoside branch.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: UGT78D2 acts in flavonol glycosylation, but the paper does not explicitly call it a 'branch point'.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The entry says 'not established', and the paper does not establish rate limitation.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The paper states UGT78D2 has been characterized as a flavonoid 3-O-glucosyltransferase and also recognizes anthocyanidins as substrates; altered products imply flavonol 3-O acceptors such as kaempferol/quercetin derivatives.

Primary_Product.reason: In ugt78d2, 3-O-glucoside derivatives k2, k3, q2 and q3 are strongly reduced, supporting these as UGT78D2-dependent products.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Directly consistent with the stated 3-O-glucosyltransferase activity and mutant metabolite profile.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Loss of UGT78D2 decreases 3-O-glucosides and increases k1, so the glucoside branch competes with accumulation of the target-product branch.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Results indicate k1 acts as endogenous inhibitor of basipetal polar auxin transport and is associated with shoot stature and delayed gravitropism.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Standard growth in sun simulator conditions is described; PAT assays used [3H]-IAA and NPA.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All listed points are described in Results: dwarf shoots, reduced cell length and apical dominance changes, complementation, altered flavonol pattern with elevated k1 and reduced PAT, and rescue by tt4 or fls1.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results directly support that ugt78d2 elevates k1, reduces basipetal auxin transport, and causes shorter shoots.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These methods are all reported and used in Results for UGT78D2: knockout analysis, complementation, double mutants, HPLC, [3H]-IAA transport, IAA quantification.

Other_Important_Info.reason: This is an accurate synthesis of the Results: loss of UGT78D2 shifts flux away from 3-O-glucosides toward 3-O-rhamnoside derivatives and k1 accumulation.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: These phenotypes are explicitly reported for ugt78d2.
